speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Be thou assured, good Cassio, I will do

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: All my abilities in thy behalf.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Good madam, do. I warrant it grieves my husband

speaker: EMILIA
text: As if the cause were his.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: O, that’s an honest fellow! Do not doubt, Cassio,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: But I will have my lord and you again

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: As friendly as you were.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Bounteous madam,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Whatever shall become of Michael Cassio,

speaker: CASSIO
text: He’s never anything but your true servant.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I know ’t. I thank you. You do love my lord;

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: You have known him long; and be you well assured

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: He shall in strangeness stand no farther off

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Than in a politic distance.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Ay, but, lady,

speaker: CASSIO
text: That policy may either last so long,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Or feed upon such nice and waterish diet,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Or breed itself so out of circumstance,

speaker: CASSIO
text: That, I being absent and my place supplied,

speaker: CASSIO
text: My general will forget my love and service.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Do not doubt that. Before Emilia here,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I give thee warrant of thy place. Assure thee,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: If I do vow a friendship, I’ll perform it

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: To the last article. My lord shall never rest:

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I’ll watch him tame and talk him out of patience;

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: His bed shall seem a school, his board a shrift;

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I’ll intermingle everything he does

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: With Cassio’s suit. Therefore be merry, Cassio,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: For thy solicitor shall rather die

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Than give thy cause away.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Madam, here comes my lord.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Madam, I’ll take my leave.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Why, stay, and hear me speak.

speaker: CASSIO
text: Madam, not now. I am very ill at ease,

speaker: CASSIO
text: Unfit for mine own purposes.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Well, do your discretion.

speaker: IAGO
text: Ha, I like not that.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What dost thou say?

speaker: IAGO
text: Nothing, my lord; or if—I know not what.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Was not that Cassio parted from my wife?

speaker: IAGO
text: Cassio, my lord? No, sure, I cannot think it

speaker: IAGO
text: That he would steal away so guiltylike,

speaker: IAGO
text: Seeing your coming.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I do believe ’twas he.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: How now, my lord?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I have been talking with a suitor here,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: A man that languishes in your displeasure.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Who is ’t you mean?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Why, your lieutenant, Cassio. Good my lord,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: If I have any grace or power to move you,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: His present reconciliation take;

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: For if he be not one that truly loves you,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: That errs in ignorance and not in cunning,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I have no judgment in an honest face.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I prithee call him back.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Went he hence now?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Yes, faith, so humbled

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: That he hath left part of his grief with me

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: To suffer with him. Good love, call him back.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Not now, sweet Desdemon. Some other time.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: But shall ’t be shortly?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The sooner, sweet, for you.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Shall ’t be tonight at supper?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: No, not tonight.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Tomorrow dinner, then?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I shall not dine at home;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I meet the captains at the citadel.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Why then tomorrow night, or Tuesday morn,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: On Tuesday noon or night; on Wednesday morn.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I prithee name the time, but let it not

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Exceed three days. In faith, he’s penitent;

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And yet his trespass, in our common reason—

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Save that, they say, the wars must make example

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Out of her best—is not almost a fault

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: T’ incur a private check. When shall he come?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Tell me, Othello. I wonder in my soul

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: What you would ask me that I should deny,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Or stand so mamm’ring on? What? Michael Cassio,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: That came a-wooing with you, and so many a time,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: When I have spoke of you dispraisingly,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Hath ta’en your part—to have so much to do

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: To bring him in! By ’r Lady, I could do much—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Prithee, no more. Let him come when he will;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I will deny thee nothing.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Why, this is not a boon!

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: ’Tis as I should entreat you wear your gloves,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Or feed on nourishing dishes, or keep you warm,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Or sue to you to do a peculiar profit

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: To your own person. Nay, when I have a suit

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Wherein I mean to touch your love indeed,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: It shall be full of poise and difficult weight,

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: And fearful to be granted.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I will deny thee nothing!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Whereon, I do beseech thee, grant me this,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To leave me but a little to myself.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Shall I deny you? No. Farewell, my lord.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Farewell, my Desdemona. I’ll come to thee straight.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Emilia, come.—Be as your fancies teach you.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Whate’er you be, I am obedient.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Excellent wretch! Perdition catch my soul

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But I do love thee! And when I love thee not,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Chaos is come again.

speaker: IAGO
text: My noble lord—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What dost thou say, Iago?

speaker: IAGO
text: Did Michael Cassio,

speaker: IAGO
text: When you wooed my lady, know of your love?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: He did, from first to last. Why dost thou ask?

speaker: IAGO
text: But for a satisfaction of my thought,

speaker: IAGO
text: No further harm.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Why of thy thought, Iago?

speaker: IAGO
text: I did not think he had been acquainted with her.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O yes, and went between us very oft.

speaker: IAGO
text: Indeed?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Indeed? Ay, indeed! Discern’st thou aught in that?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Is he not honest?

speaker: IAGO
text: Honest, my lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Honest—ay, honest.

speaker: IAGO
text: My lord, for aught I know.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What dost thou think?

speaker: IAGO
text: Think, my lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: “Think, my lord?” By heaven, thou echo’st me

speaker: OTHELLO
text: As if there were some monster in thy thought

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Too hideous to be shown. Thou dost mean something.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I heard thee say even now, thou lik’st not that,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: When Cassio left my wife. What didst not like?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And when I told thee he was of my counsel

speaker: OTHELLO
text: In my whole course of wooing, thou cried’st “Indeed?”

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And didst contract and purse thy brow together

speaker: OTHELLO
text: As if thou then hadst shut up in thy brain

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Some horrible conceit. If thou dost love me,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Show me thy thought.

speaker: IAGO
text: My lord, you know I love you.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I think thou dost;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And for I know thou ’rt full of love and honesty

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And weigh’st thy words before thou giv’st them breath,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Therefore these stops of thine fright me the more.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For such things in a false, disloyal knave

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Are tricks of custom; but in a man that’s just,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: They’re close dilations working from the heart

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That passion cannot rule.

speaker: IAGO
text: For Michael Cassio,

speaker: IAGO
text: I dare be sworn I think that he is honest.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I think so too.

speaker: IAGO
text: Men should be what they seem;

speaker: IAGO
text: Or those that be not, would they might seem none!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Certain, men should be what they seem.

speaker: IAGO
text: Why then, I think Cassio’s an honest man.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nay, yet there’s more in this.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I prithee speak to me as to thy thinkings,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: As thou dost ruminate, and give thy worst of thoughts

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The worst of words.

speaker: IAGO
text: Good my lord, pardon me.

speaker: IAGO
text: Though I am bound to every act of duty,

speaker: IAGO
text: I am not bound to that all slaves are free to.

speaker: IAGO
text: Utter my thoughts? Why, say they are vile and false—

speaker: IAGO
text: As where’s that palace whereinto foul things

speaker: IAGO
text: Sometimes intrude not? Who has that breast so pure

speaker: IAGO
text: But some uncleanly apprehensions

speaker: IAGO
text: Keep leets and law days and in sessions sit

speaker: IAGO
text: With meditations lawful?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Thou dost conspire against thy friend, Iago,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If thou but think’st him wronged and mak’st his ear

speaker: OTHELLO
text: A stranger to thy thoughts.

speaker: IAGO
text: I do beseech you,

speaker: IAGO
text: Though I perchance am vicious in my guess—

speaker: IAGO
text: As, I confess, it is my nature’s plague

speaker: IAGO
text: To spy into abuses, and oft my jealousy

speaker: IAGO
text: Shapes faults that are not—that your wisdom

speaker: IAGO
text: From one that so imperfectly conceits

speaker: IAGO
text: Would take no notice, nor build yourself a trouble

speaker: IAGO
text: Out of his scattering and unsure observance.

speaker: IAGO
text: It were not for your quiet nor your good,

speaker: IAGO
text: Nor for my manhood, honesty, and wisdom,

speaker: IAGO
text: To let you know my thoughts.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What dost thou mean?

speaker: IAGO
text: Good name in man and woman, dear my lord,

speaker: IAGO
text: Is the immediate jewel of their souls.

speaker: IAGO
text: Who steals my purse steals trash. ’Tis something, nothing;

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Twas mine, ’tis his, and has been slave to thousands.

speaker: IAGO
text: But he that filches from me my good name

speaker: IAGO
text: Robs me of that which not enriches him

speaker: IAGO
text: And makes me poor indeed.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: By heaven, I’ll know thy thoughts.

speaker: IAGO
text: You cannot, if my heart were in your hand,

speaker: IAGO
text: Nor shall not, whilst ’tis in my custody.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ha?

speaker: IAGO
text: O, beware, my lord, of jealousy!

speaker: IAGO
text: It is the green-eyed monster which doth mock

speaker: IAGO
text: The meat it feeds on. That cuckold lives in bliss

speaker: IAGO
text: Who, certain of his fate, loves not his wronger;

speaker: IAGO
text: But O, what damnèd minutes tells he o’er

speaker: IAGO
text: Who dotes, yet doubts; suspects, yet strongly loves!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O misery!

speaker: IAGO
text: Poor and content is rich, and rich enough;

speaker: IAGO
text: But riches fineless is as poor as winter

speaker: IAGO
text: To him that ever fears he shall be poor.

speaker: IAGO
text: Good God, the souls of all my tribe defend

speaker: IAGO
text: From jealousy!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Why, why is this?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Think’st thou I’d make a life of jealousy,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To follow still the changes of the moon

speaker: OTHELLO
text: With fresh suspicions? No. To be once in doubt

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Is once to be resolved. Exchange me for a goat

speaker: OTHELLO
text: When I shall turn the business of my soul

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To such exsufflicate and blown surmises,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Matching thy inference. ’Tis not to make me jealous

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To say my wife is fair, feeds well, loves company,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Is free of speech, sings, plays, and dances well.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Where virtue is, these are more virtuous.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nor from mine own weak merits will I draw

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The smallest fear or doubt of her revolt,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For she had eyes, and chose me. No, Iago,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’ll see before I doubt; when I doubt, prove;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And on the proof, there is no more but this:

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Away at once with love or jealousy.

speaker: IAGO
text: I am glad of this, for now I shall have reason

speaker: IAGO
text: To show the love and duty that I bear you

speaker: IAGO
text: With franker spirit. Therefore, as I am bound,

speaker: IAGO
text: Receive it from me. I speak not yet of proof.

speaker: IAGO
text: Look to your wife; observe her well with Cassio;

speaker: IAGO
text: Wear your eyes thus, not jealous nor secure.

speaker: IAGO
text: I would not have your free and noble nature,

speaker: IAGO
text: Out of self-bounty, be abused. Look to ’t.

speaker: IAGO
text: I know our country disposition well.

speaker: IAGO
text: In Venice they do let God see the pranks

speaker: IAGO
text: They dare not show their husbands. Their best conscience

speaker: IAGO
text: Is not to leave ’t undone, but keep ’t unknown.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Dost thou say so?

speaker: IAGO
text: She did deceive her father, marrying you,

speaker: IAGO
text: And when she seemed to shake and fear your looks,

speaker: IAGO
text: She loved them most.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And so she did.

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, go to, then!

speaker: IAGO
text: She that, so young, could give out such a seeming,

speaker: IAGO
text: To seel her father’s eyes up close as oak,

speaker: IAGO
text: He thought ’twas witchcraft! But I am much to blame.

speaker: IAGO
text: I humbly do beseech you of your pardon

speaker: IAGO
text: For too much loving you.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I am bound to thee forever.

speaker: IAGO
text: I see this hath a little dashed your spirits.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Not a jot, not a jot.

speaker: IAGO
text: I’ faith, I fear it has.

speaker: IAGO
text: I hope you will consider what is spoke

speaker: IAGO
text: Comes from my love. But I do see you’re moved.

speaker: IAGO
text: I am to pray you not to strain my speech

speaker: IAGO
text: To grosser issues nor to larger reach

speaker: IAGO
text: Than to suspicion.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I will not.

speaker: IAGO
text: Should you do so, my lord,

speaker: IAGO
text: My speech should fall into such vile success

speaker: IAGO
text: As my thoughts aim not at. Cassio’s my worthy friend.

speaker: IAGO
text: My lord, I see you’re moved.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: No, not much moved.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I do not think but Desdemona’s honest.

speaker: IAGO
text: Long live she so! And long live you to think so!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And yet, how nature erring from itself—

speaker: IAGO
text: Ay, there’s the point. As, to be bold with you,

speaker: IAGO
text: Not to affect many proposèd matches

speaker: IAGO
text: Of her own clime, complexion, and degree,

speaker: IAGO
text: Whereto we see in all things nature tends—

speaker: IAGO
text: Foh! One may smell in such a will most rank,

speaker: IAGO
text: Foul disproportion, thoughts unnatural—

speaker: IAGO
text: But pardon me—I do not in position

speaker: IAGO
text: Distinctly speak of her, though I may fear

speaker: IAGO
text: Her will, recoiling to her better judgment,

speaker: IAGO
text: May fall to match you with her country forms

speaker: IAGO
text: And happily repent.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Farewell, farewell!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If more thou dost perceive, let me know more.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Set on thy wife to observe. Leave me, Iago.

speaker: IAGO
text: My lord, I take my leave.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Why did I marry? This honest creature doubtless

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Sees and knows more, much more, than he unfolds.

speaker: IAGO
text: My lord, I would I might entreat your Honor

speaker: IAGO
text: To scan this thing no farther. Leave it to time.

speaker: IAGO
text: Although ’tis fit that Cassio have his place—

speaker: IAGO
text: For sure he fills it up with great ability—

speaker: IAGO
text: Yet, if you please to hold him off awhile,

speaker: IAGO
text: You shall by that perceive him and his means.

speaker: IAGO
text: Note if your lady strain his entertainment

speaker: IAGO
text: With any strong or vehement importunity.

speaker: IAGO
text: Much will be seen in that. In the meantime,

speaker: IAGO
text: Let me be thought too busy in my fears—

speaker: IAGO
text: As worthy cause I have to fear I am—

speaker: IAGO
text: And hold her free, I do beseech your Honor.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Fear not my government.

speaker: IAGO
text: I once more take my leave.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: This fellow’s of exceeding honesty,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And knows all qualities with a learnèd spirit

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Of human dealings. If I do prove her haggard,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Though that her jesses were my dear heartstrings,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’d whistle her off and let her down the wind

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To prey at fortune. Haply, for I am black

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And have not those soft parts of conversation

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That chamberers have, or for I am declined

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Into the vale of years—yet that’s not much—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: She’s gone, I am abused, and my relief

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Must be to loathe her. O curse of marriage,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That we can call these delicate creatures ours

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And not their appetites! I had rather be a toad

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And live upon the vapor of a dungeon

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Than keep a corner in the thing I love

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For others’ uses. Yet ’tis the plague of great ones;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Prerogatived are they less than the base.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Tis destiny unshunnable, like death.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Even then this forkèd plague is fated to us

speaker: OTHELLO
text: When we do quicken. Look where she comes.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If she be false, heaven mocks itself!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’ll not believe ’t.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: How now, my dear Othello?

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Your dinner, and the generous islanders

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: By you invited, do attend your presence.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I am to blame.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Why do you speak so faintly? Are you not well?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I have a pain upon my forehead, here.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Faith, that’s with watching. ’Twill away again.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: Let me but bind it hard; within this hour

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: It will be well.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Your napkin is too little.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Let it alone.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Come, I’ll go in with you.

speaker: DESDEMONA
text: I am very sorry that you are not well.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I am glad I have found this napkin.

speaker: EMILIA
text: This was her first remembrance from the Moor.

speaker: EMILIA
text: My wayward husband hath a hundred times

speaker: EMILIA
text: Wooed me to steal it. But she so loves the token

speaker: EMILIA
text: (For he conjured her she should ever keep it)

speaker: EMILIA
text: That she reserves it evermore about her

speaker: EMILIA
text: To kiss and talk to. I’ll have the work ta’en out

speaker: EMILIA
text: And give ’t Iago. What he will do with it

speaker: EMILIA
text: Heaven knows, not I.

speaker: EMILIA
text: I nothing but to please his fantasy.

speaker: IAGO
text: How now? What do you here alone?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Do not you chide. I have a thing for you.

speaker: IAGO
text: You have a thing for me? It is a common thing—

speaker: EMILIA
text: Ha?

speaker: IAGO
text: To have a foolish wife.

speaker: EMILIA
text: O, is that all? What will you give me now

speaker: EMILIA
text: For that same handkerchief?

speaker: IAGO
text: What handkerchief?

speaker: EMILIA
text: What handkerchief?

speaker: EMILIA
text: Why, that the Moor first gave to Desdemona,

speaker: EMILIA
text: That which so often you did bid me steal.

speaker: IAGO
text: Hast stol’n it from her?

speaker: EMILIA
text: No, faith, she let it drop by negligence,

speaker: EMILIA
text: And to th’ advantage I, being here, took ’t up.

speaker: EMILIA
text: Look, here ’tis.

speaker: IAGO
text: A good wench! Give it me.

speaker: EMILIA
text: What will you do with ’t, that you have been so earnest

speaker: EMILIA
text: To have me filch it?

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, what is that to you?

speaker: EMILIA
text: If it be not for some purpose of import,

speaker: EMILIA
text: Give ’t me again. Poor lady, she’ll run mad

speaker: EMILIA
text: When she shall lack it.

speaker: IAGO
text: Be not acknown on ’t.

speaker: IAGO
text: I have use for it. Go, leave me.

speaker: IAGO
text: I will in Cassio’s lodging lose this napkin

speaker: IAGO
text: And let him find it. Trifles light as air

speaker: IAGO
text: Are to the jealous confirmations strong

speaker: IAGO
text: As proofs of holy writ. This may do something.

speaker: IAGO
text: The Moor already changes with my poison;

speaker: IAGO
text: Dangerous conceits are in their natures poisons,

speaker: IAGO
text: Which at the first are scarce found to distaste,

speaker: IAGO
text: But with a little act upon the blood

speaker: IAGO
text: Burn like the mines of sulfur.

speaker: IAGO
text: I did say so.

speaker: IAGO
text: Look where he comes. Not poppy nor mandragora

speaker: IAGO
text: Nor all the drowsy syrups of the world

speaker: IAGO
text: Shall ever medicine thee to that sweet sleep

speaker: IAGO
text: Which thou owedst yesterday.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ha, ha, false to me?

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, how now, general? No more of that!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Avaunt! Begone! Thou hast set me on the rack.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I swear ’tis better to be much abused

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Than but to know ’t a little.

speaker: IAGO
text: How now, my lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: What sense had I of her stol’n hours of lust?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I saw ’t not, thought it not; it harmed not me.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I slept the next night well, fed well, was free and merry.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I found not Cassio’s kisses on her lips.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: He that is robbed, not wanting what is stol’n,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Let him not know ’t, and he’s not robbed at all.

speaker: IAGO
text: I am sorry to hear this.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I had been happy if the general camp,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Pioners and all, had tasted her sweet body,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: So I had nothing known. O, now, forever

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Farewell the tranquil mind! Farewell content!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Farewell the plumèd troops and the big wars

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That makes ambition virtue! O, farewell!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Farewell the neighing steed and the shrill trump,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The spirit-stirring drum, th’ ear-piercing fife,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: The royal banner, and all quality,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Pride, pomp, and circumstance of glorious war!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And O you mortal engines, whose rude throats

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Th’ immortal Jove’s dread clamors counterfeit,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Farewell! Othello’s occupation’s gone!

speaker: IAGO
text: Is ’t possible, my lord?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Villain, be sure thou prove my love a whore!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Be sure of it. Give me the ocular proof,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Or, by the worth of mine eternal soul,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Thou hadst been better have been born a dog

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Than answer my waked wrath.

speaker: IAGO
text: Is ’t come to this?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Make me to see ’t, or at the least so prove it

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That the probation bear no hinge nor loop

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To hang a doubt on, or woe upon thy life!

speaker: IAGO
text: My noble lord—

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If thou dost slander her and torture me,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Never pray more. Abandon all remorse;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: On horror’s head horrors accumulate;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Do deeds to make heaven weep, all Earth amazed;

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For nothing canst thou to damnation add

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Greater than that.

speaker: IAGO
text: O grace! O heaven forgive me!

speaker: IAGO
text: Are you a man? Have you a soul or sense?

speaker: IAGO
text: God b’ wi’ you. Take mine office.—O wretched fool,

speaker: IAGO
text: That liv’st to make thine honesty a vice!—

speaker: IAGO
text: O monstrous world! Take note, take note, O world:

speaker: IAGO
text: To be direct and honest is not safe.—

speaker: IAGO
text: I thank you for this profit, and from hence

speaker: IAGO
text: I’ll love no friend, sith love breeds such offense.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Nay, stay. Thou shouldst be honest.

speaker: IAGO
text: I should be wise; for honesty’s a fool

speaker: IAGO
text: And loses that it works for.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: By the world,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I think my wife be honest and think she is not.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I think that thou art just and think thou art not.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’ll have some proof! Her name, that was as fresh

speaker: OTHELLO
text: As Dian’s visage, is now begrimed and black

speaker: OTHELLO
text: As mine own face. If there be cords, or knives,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Poison, or fire, or suffocating streams,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’ll not endure it. Would I were satisfied!

speaker: IAGO
text: I see you are eaten up with passion.

speaker: IAGO
text: I do repent me that I put it to you.

speaker: IAGO
text: You would be satisfied?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Would? Nay, and I will.

speaker: IAGO
text: And may; but how? How satisfied, my lord?

speaker: IAGO
text: Would you, the supervisor, grossly gape on,

speaker: IAGO
text: Behold her topped?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Death and damnation! O!

speaker: IAGO
text: It were a tedious difficulty, I think,

speaker: IAGO
text: To bring them to that prospect. Damn them then

speaker: IAGO
text: If ever mortal eyes do see them bolster

speaker: IAGO
text: More than their own! What then? How then?

speaker: IAGO
text: What shall I say? Where’s satisfaction?

speaker: IAGO
text: It is impossible you should see this,

speaker: IAGO
text: Were they as prime as goats, as hot as monkeys,

speaker: IAGO
text: As salt as wolves in pride, and fools as gross

speaker: IAGO
text: As ignorance made drunk. But yet I say,

speaker: IAGO
text: If imputation and strong circumstances

speaker: IAGO
text: Which lead directly to the door of truth

speaker: IAGO
text: Will give you satisfaction, you might have ’t.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Give me a living reason she’s disloyal.

speaker: IAGO
text: I do not like the office,

speaker: IAGO
text: But sith I am entered in this cause so far,

speaker: IAGO
text: Pricked to ’t by foolish honesty and love,

speaker: IAGO
text: I will go on. I lay with Cassio lately,

speaker: IAGO
text: And being troubled with a raging tooth

speaker: IAGO
text: I could not sleep. There are a kind of men

speaker: IAGO
text: So loose of soul that in their sleeps will mutter

speaker: IAGO
text: Their affairs. One of this kind is Cassio.

speaker: IAGO
text: In sleep I heard him say “Sweet Desdemona,

speaker: IAGO
text: Let us be wary, let us hide our loves.”

speaker: IAGO
text: And then, sir, would he gripe and wring my hand,

speaker: IAGO
text: Cry “O sweet creature!” then kiss me hard,

speaker: IAGO
text: As if he plucked up kisses by the roots

speaker: IAGO
text: That grew upon my lips; then laid his leg

speaker: IAGO
text: O’er my thigh, and sighed, and kissed, and then

speaker: IAGO
text: Cried “Cursèd fate that gave thee to the Moor!”

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O monstrous! Monstrous!

speaker: IAGO
text: Nay, this was but his dream.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: But this denoted a foregone conclusion.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Tis a shrewd doubt, though it be but a dream.

speaker: IAGO
text: And this may help to thicken other proofs

speaker: IAGO
text: That do demonstrate thinly.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I’ll tear her all to pieces.

speaker: IAGO
text: Nay, but be wise. Yet we see nothing done.

speaker: IAGO
text: She may be honest yet. Tell me but this:

speaker: IAGO
text: Have you not sometimes seen a handkerchief

speaker: IAGO
text: Spotted with strawberries in your wife’s hand?

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I gave her such a one. ’Twas my first gift.

speaker: IAGO
text: I know not that; but such a handkerchief—

speaker: IAGO
text: I am sure it was your wife’s—did I today

speaker: IAGO
text: See Cassio wipe his beard with.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: If it be that—

speaker: IAGO
text: If it be that, or any that was hers,

speaker: IAGO
text: It speaks against her with the other proofs.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, that the slave had forty thousand lives!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: One is too poor, too weak for my revenge.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Now do I see ’tis true. Look here, Iago,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: All my fond love thus do I blow to heaven.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: ’Tis gone.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Arise, black vengeance, from the hollow hell!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Yield up, O love, thy crown and hearted throne

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To tyrannous hate! Swell, bosom, with thy fraught,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For ’tis of aspics’ tongues!

speaker: IAGO
text: Yet be content.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: O, blood, blood, blood!

speaker: IAGO
text: Patience, I say. Your mind perhaps may change.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Never, Iago. Like to the Pontic Sea,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Whose icy current and compulsive course

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Ne’er feels retiring ebb, but keeps due on

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To the Propontic and the Hellespont,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Even so my bloody thoughts, with violent pace

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Shall ne’er look back, ne’er ebb to humble love,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Till that a capable and wide revenge

speaker: OTHELLO
text:
Swallow them up.
                 
                He
                 
                kneels
                .
               Now by yond marble heaven,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: In the due reverence of a sacred vow,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I here engage my words.

speaker: IAGO
text: Do not rise yet.

speaker: IAGO
text: Witness, you ever-burning lights above,

speaker: IAGO
text: You elements that clip us round about,

speaker: IAGO
text: Witness that here Iago doth give up

speaker: IAGO
text: The execution of his wit, hands, heart

speaker: IAGO
text: To wronged Othello’s service! Let him command,

speaker: IAGO
text: And to obey shall be in me remorse,

speaker: IAGO
text: What bloody business ever.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: I greet thy love

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Not with vain thanks but with acceptance bounteous,

speaker: OTHELLO
text: And will upon the instant put thee to ’t.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Within these three days let me hear thee say

speaker: OTHELLO
text: That Cassio’s not alive.

speaker: IAGO
text: My friend is dead.

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Tis done at your request. But let her live.

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Damn her, lewd minx! O, damn her, damn her!

speaker: OTHELLO
text: Come, go with me apart. I will withdraw

speaker: OTHELLO
text: To furnish me with some swift means of death

speaker: OTHELLO
text: For the fair devil. Now art thou my lieutenant.

speaker: IAGO
text: I am your own forever.
